content.full_text:
-    A                    California
     [SEAL]                Fair Political
                          Practices Commission


                                        November 9, 1988


Greg Morris, President
California Coalition for
Trucking Deregulation
P.O. Box 364
Newark, CA  94560

                                        RE:  Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. A-88-379

Dear Mr. Morris:

     You have requested advice regarding the lobbying provisions
of the Political Reform Act of 1974 (the "Act").1/

                              QUESTION

     Are dues and voluntary payments received by the California
Coalition for Trucking Deregulation, a lobbyist employer,
reportable under the lobbying provisions of the Act if the dues
and payments are made by members of the Coalition which are
lobbyist employers?

                             CONCLUSION

     Regular dues paid by members of a bona fide association,
some portion of which is used for lobbying purposes, are not
reportable by the association.  Such payments also are not
required to be reported by the member.  However, payments,
other than dues payments, made to the association by an
individual member who is a lobbyist employer for purposes of
assisting the association in attempting to influence
legislative or administrative action must be reported by the
member on Form 635, Part III, Section D.  Payments, other than
regular dues payments, made by a member who is not a lobbyist
employer must be reported by the member for each quarter in
which total payments of $5,000 are made.


_________________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Morris, Greg
Page Two

FACTS

The California Coalition for Trucking Deregulation is a
non-profit corporation engaged in lobbying both the state
Legislature and the Public Utilities Commission. The Coalition
is made up of approximately 150 member companies and
associations. Some of these companies also employ lobbyists in
California.

Funding for the Coalition is derived from dues and
voluntary payments. Some of the voluntary payments may total
$5,000 in a calendar quarter. Approximately 30% to 40% of the
Coalition's funds are used for lobbying activity.

ANALYSIS

Regulation 18616(g)(3) (copy enclosed) excludes dues or
similar payments made by any person for membership in a bona
fide association, including any federation, confederation or
trade, labor or membership organization, some portion of which
is used to influence legislative or administrative action, from
the definition of payments to influence legislative or
administrative action. Therefore, dues payments are not
reportable by the person who makes the payments or by the
organization which receives the payments.

Regulation 18616(f) requires all persons who file lobbyist
employer reports to report the total of all payments to
influence legislative or administrative action. Therefore, all
members which are lobbyist employers must report all payments,
other than regular dues payments, to the Coalition for purposes
of attempting to influence legislative or administrative
action. The payments are reported by the members on their
lobbyist employer reports, Form 635, Part III, Section D.

Members who are not lobbyist employers also may be required
to disclose lobbying payments made to the Coalition. All
payments, other than dues or similar payments for membership in
a bona fide association which are made to influence legislative
or administrative action must be combined and a report filed if
the total is $5,000 or more in a calendar quarter. However, if
the only reportable payments made are activity expenses, as
defined by Section 86111(a), no report is required.
(Regulation 18616(i).)

Section 86111(a) defines an activity expense as any expense
incurred or payment made by a lobbyist, lobbying firm, lobbyist
employer or a person spending $5,000 in a calendar quarter to
influence legislative or administrative action, which benefits
in whole or in part any elective state official, legislative
official, agency official, state candidate, or a member of the
immediate family of one of these individuals. Activity
expenses include gifts, honoraria, consulting fees, salaries,
and any other form of compensation but do not include campaign
contributions.

Morris, Greg
Page Three

If you have additional questions, please call me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths,
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

California Coalition
for Trucking Deregulation

September 26, 1988


Secretary of State
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95807

Dear FPPC:

     The California Coalition for Trucking Deregulation is a non-profit
California corporation engaged in advocacy with both the state legislature and the
Public Utilities Commission through lobbyists retained by the Coalition. The
Coalition is made up of approximately 150 member companies and associations.
Some of these companies also employ lobbyists in California.

     Funding for the Coalition's activities is exclusively through two sources,
dues and voluntary contributions. Dues range from $100 to $250 and voluntary
contributions from $500 to $25,000. Approximately 30% to 40% of the
Coalition's funds are spent on activities reportable under California law. More
precise determination of funds so spent could be easily made.

     A question has arisen which motivates this letter. For companies which
are members of the Coalition and Employers of Lobbyists under California law,
is all or some portion of 1.)Coalition dues, and 2.)a voluntary contribution
reportable to your Commission.

     The courtesy of an early reply is requested. Thank you.

Very truly yours,


Greg Morris
President


California Coalition
for Trucking Deregulation
P. O. Box 364
Newark, CA 94560

883349

California Coalition
for Trucking Deregulation

September 26, 1988


Secretary of State
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA   95807

Dear FPPC:

The California Coalition for Trucking Deregulation is a non-profit
California corporation engaged in advocacy with both the state legislature and the
Public Utilities Commission through lobbyists retained by the Coalition. The
Coalition is made up of approximately 150 member companies and associations.
Some of these companies also employ lobbyists in California.

Funding for the Coalition's activities is exclusively through two sources,
dues and voluntary contributions. Dues range from $100 to $250 and voluntary
contributions from $500 to $25,000. Approximately 30% to 40% of the
Coalition's funds are spent on activities reportable under California law. More
precise determination of funds so spent could be easily made.

A question has arisen which motivates this letter. For companies which
are members of the Coalition and Employers of Lobbyists under California law,
is all or some portion of 1.)Coalition dues, and 2.)a voluntary contribution
reportable to your Commission.

The courtesy of an early reply is requested. Thank you.

Very truly yours,

Greg Morris
President

similar payments made by any person for membership in a bona 
fide association, including any federation, confederation or 
trade, labor or membership organization, some portion of which 
is used to influence legislative or administrative action, from

administrative action.

employer reports to report the total of all payments to 
influence legislative or administrative action. 
Therefore, all 
members which are lobbyist employers must report payments,

of attempting to influence legislative or administrative 
action. 
The payments are reported by the members on their 
lobbyist employer reports, Form 635, Part III, section D. 
Members who are not lobbyist employers also may be required 
to disclose lobbying payments made to the Coalition. 
All 
payments, other than dues or similar payments for membership in

the only reportable payments made are activity expenses, as

Activity 
expenses include gifts, honoraria, consulting fees, salaries,
